---
title: "Quest 4: Loops - Repeat with Confidence"
subtitle: "Use loop, while, and for"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🔄 QUEST 4 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/04-loops.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/04-loops.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Loops help you repeat work without copy-pasting code.

## 💡 Three Common Loops

```rust
fn main() {
    for n in 1..=3 {
        println!("for loop: {}", n);
    }

    let mut count = 0;
    while count < 2 {
        println!("while loop: {}", count);
        count += 1;
    }
}
```

## 👨‍💻 Example with `loop`

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut n = 0;

    loop {
        n += 1;
        println!("n = {}", n);

        if n == 3 {
            break;
        }
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Use a `for` loop to print numbers 1 through 5 and their squares.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    for n in 1..=5 {
        println!("{} squared is {}", n, n * n);
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 4 Complete
You practiced three Rust loop styles.
Next: [Quest 5: Vectors](05-lists.qmd)
:::
